name: pkl-engineering-workflow-routing
description: Concrete routing cases for the dedicated Pkl Engineering plugin's implementation, setup, specification, pattern-catalog, and repository-proof boundaries.

cases.prompt: Change this Pkl configuration contract, render the consumer JSON, and prove its constraints and tests still pass.

observedRoute.summary: Pkl edits can be treated as text transformation and rendered without evaluating the real entry module or its tests.
observedRoute.risk: The output may look correct while required properties, constraints, snapshots, or evaluator capabilities are wrong.

allowedActions: Inspect the workspace and Pkl version through the bundled facade.
Evaluate the real consumer module under an explicit policy.
Run focused Pkl tests in the non-mutating sandbox.
Render the requested JSON from the evaluated typed model.
forbiddenActions: Treat successful formatting as proof of the configuration contract.
Hand-edit generated JSON as a second source of truth.
Enable environment, HTTPS, reader, or command capabilities implicitly.
cases.recoveryExpectation: A good run records exact evaluation, test, and render commands plus the declared evaluator policy.
cases.notes: This separates day-to-day Pkl implementation from setup and research work.
cases.productiveOutcome: Pkl changes are enforced through evaluated types and tests before consumer artifacts are rendered.
verificationEvidence: The real entry module evaluates successfully or reports a precise failure.
Focused Pkl tests pass without unreviewed snapshot writes.
Rendered JSON is derived from the typed Pkl entry module.

cases.prompt: Set up Pkl and the LSP for this repository in VS Code, then explain the available JSON/YAML rendering and build tooling.

observedRoute.summary: Setup guidance can guess package names, Java requirements, editor settings, or renderer support without checking the installed state.
observedRoute.risk: The editor and CLI drift onto incompatible versions and repository validation works differently from IDE diagnostics.

allowedActions: Run the non-mutating tooling doctor.
Use the official VS Code extension and current versioned installation documentation.
Point the editor at the same Pkl CLI used by repository proof.
Describe renderers and transformations as derived outputs from an evaluated model.
forbiddenActions: Run package-manager or extension installation without explicit user intent.
Treat standalone pkl-lsp as the preferred end-user surface when an official editor plugin exists.
Hard-code a Java or LSP version without checking current official documentation.
cases.recoveryExpectation: A good run leaves observable CLI/editor version evidence and an explicit project-sync verification step.
cases.notes: This case covers installation, LSP, rendering, and tooling discovery.
cases.productiveOutcome: The editor, CLI, and repository proof loop use compatible, inspectable Pkl tooling.
verificationEvidence: The tooling doctor reports resolved Pkl, Java, LSP, editor, and project state.
Installation guidance links to first-party documentation and uses an explicit plan.
The final setup includes a CLI evaluation or test outside the editor.

cases.prompt: Specify a Pkl model that reads external YAML resources, emits JSON, and tell me which official repositories define the relevant APIs and examples.

observedRoute.summary: A specification request can collapse into implementation snippets without naming entry modules, resource schemes, consumers, or source authority.
observedRoute.risk: Dynamic inputs and external capabilities leak into downstream code and repository examples are mistaken for normative API contracts.

allowedActions: Inventory consumers, entry modules, templates, resources, outputs, and proof.
Convert parsed YAML from Dynamic to a typed boundary.
Route semantics to versioned docs and implementation questions to exact first-party commits.
Use the locked source index tool when repository content is needed.
forbiddenActions: Treat YAML or JSON as a second authored configuration authority.
Allow broad environment, filesystem, HTTPS, or reader access without naming it.
Copy upstream documentation into the local skill as canonical policy.
cases.recoveryExpectation: A good result is a typed boundary specification with least-privilege resource capabilities and traceable first-party provenance.
cases.notes: This separates specification and source understanding from direct Pkl implementation.
cases.productiveOutcome: The Pkl resource contract is typed, capability-bounded, and backed by exact first-party sources.
verificationEvidence: The specification distinguishes modules from resources and names URI schemes.
Dynamic YAML data is converted before downstream use.
Non-obvious claims cite versioned docs or an exact repository commit.

cases.prompt: Which Pkl pattern should I use for a package API with extension points, multiple output files, and a custom renderer?

observedRoute.summary: Pattern advice can load a monolithic language guide or answer from memory without distinguishing everyday and capability-expanding designs.
observedRoute.risk: Advanced extension and output capabilities are applied casually, with no version check or focused proof.

allowedActions: Load only the advanced-pattern catalog for this request.
Name the invariant or consumer problem for each selected pattern.
Confirm syntax against version-matched official sources.
Prove package API tests and rendered file behavior.
forbiddenActions: Load both catalogs when only advanced patterns are relevant.
Apply open extension points or custom converters without a stated need.
Present a proposal from pkl-evolution as already-shipped behavior.
cases.recoveryExpectation: A good answer routes to exact advanced patterns, documents their capabilities, and names evaluation/package proof.
cases.notes: This is the progressive-disclosure case for the lean catalog skill.
cases.productiveOutcome: Agents select version-supported Pkl patterns without paying for unrelated catalog context.
verificationEvidence: Only the advanced catalog is needed for the request.
Each selected pattern maps to a stated consumer or invariant.
The plan includes package API tests and rendered-output proof.

cases.prompt: Finish a Pkl change and let the repository hooks prove formatting, configuration evaluation, and assertions.

observedRoute.summary: A Stop hook can guess that every changed Pkl module is directly evaluable or run tests in place where snapshots may be written.
observedRoute.risk: Valid abstract/template modules fail spuriously, or hook execution mutates expected/actual test files.

allowedActions: Format-check only changed Pkl-owned files.
Evaluate only modules declared in .intelligence/pkl-entrypoints.
Delegate testing to the pkl-engineering sandboxed test facade.
Skip with an actionable reason when no explicit evaluation roots exist.
forbiddenActions: Guess that abstract modules and templates can be evaluated directly.
Run snapshot-capable Pkl tests directly in the source workspace.
Report success when the delegated Pkl facade fails.
cases.recoveryExpectation: A good hook run is deterministic, non-mutating, and explicit about skipped or evaluated roots.
cases.notes: This tests the hook adapter and implementation ownership boundary.
cases.productiveOutcome: Pkl Stop hooks provide reliable formatting, evaluation, and assertion evidence without guessing or source mutations.
verificationEvidence: Evaluation uses the repository entrypoint declaration.
Tests run through the sandboxed facade.
